user_query:
I’m preparing product requirements for stadium and arena deployments and need a small, verifiable research bundle on intelligibility targets and safe sound levels from authoritative sources. Please build this entirely in the local workspace with the following outputs:

1) Internet discovery and traceability
- Use a search engine programmatically (no hardcoded URLs) to find and collect three authoritative documents:
  a) One U.S. regulatory or health guidance source on occupational/audience noise exposure from OSHA (domain: osha.gov) or NIOSH (domain: cdc.gov/niosh).
  b) The U.S. Department of Defense Unified Facilities Criteria document titled “Unified Facilities Criteria (UFC) 4-021-01, Mass Notification Systems,” accessible via the Whole Building Design Guide (domain: wbdg.org).
  c) One non-U.S. official guidance source on event/venue noise or safety from HSE (domain: hse.gov.uk), EU-OSHA (domain: osha.europa.eu), or WHO (domain: who.int).
- Save a log of the exact queries and candidate URLs considered to logs/search_queries.json with fields: query, timestamp, candidates (array of {title, url}).
- Do not use blogs, forums, or paywalled content. At least one source must be a web HTML page (not PDF), and at least one must be a PDF.

2) Download and extraction
- Download the three selected resources to data/raw/ using safe, filesystem-friendly filenames that include the source domain and a short title (e.g., data/raw/wbdg_org_ufc_4_021_01.pdf).
- Extract the main text from each resource to data/extracted/ as UTF-8 .txt files with matching basenames (e.g., data/extracted/wbdg_org_ufc_4_021_01.txt). For HTML pages, extract readable body content; for PDFs, extract text pages.

3) Index and metrics artifacts
- Create a structured index at output/venue_audio_guidance_index.csv with one row per resource and columns: id, source_org, domain, doc_title, publication_year (if detectable), doc_type (regulatory|standard|technical_guidance), target_context (workers|audience|mass_notification|spectator_safety), topic_tags (semicolon-separated), url, local_path_raw, local_path_text, content_sha256.
- From each extracted text, programmatically scan for quantitative mentions relevant to large-venue audio: numbers adjacent to tokens like "dB", "dBA", "LAeq", "exposure", "STI", "STIPA", "CIS", and "intelligibility". Save a consolidated JSON at output/metrics_extracted.json with an object per document: {id, doc_title, domain, levels_dB: [{value, unit, snippet}], intelligibility_metrics: [{metric, value, snippet}], exposure_durations: [{value, unit, snippet}]}. Snippets should be short (<=200 chars) excerpts from the extracted text around the match.

4) Design brief
- Write a concise synthesis at output/venue_audio_design_brief.md with these sections:
  - Sources: bullet list of the three documents with title, organization, and domain.
  - Quantitative highlights: bullet points summarizing the extracted numeric items that are relevant to product design for large venues (e.g., SPL limits, LAeq references, intelligibility thresholds), each bullet naming the source id and quoting the snippet.
  - Implications for product design: 3–6 bullets connecting highlights to requirements for loudspeaker capability, system tuning, and compliance (e.g., peak/continuous output vs. exposure limits, STI targets for mass notification).
  - Appendix: a link to venue_audio_guidance_index.csv and metrics_extracted.json.

Acceptance constraints
- The three sources must satisfy the domain/title constraints above and be saved locally. At least one must be HTML and at least one must be PDF.
- output/metrics_extracted.json must contain at least one 'levels_dB' item (e.g., a value with dB/dBA/LAeq) from any document and at least one 'intelligibility_metrics' item (e.g., STI/STIPA/CIS) from any document—UFC 4-021-01 is expected to satisfy the intelligibility requirement.
- All paths must be relative and reside under the workspace: logs/, data/raw/, data/extracted/, output/.

Deliver exactly these artifacts: logs/search_queries.json, all files under data/raw/ and data/extracted/, output/venue_audio_guidance_index.csv, output/metrics_extracted.json, and output/venue_audio_design_brief.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
